Subject: CHM
Title: Chemistry In Modern Society
Description:
111 Chemistry in Modern Society (4)

For non-science majors. Considers both nature of basic chemical processes and ways that chemistry affects our society. Introduction to how scientists approach problems and make decisions. 3 Lec. IVB. PA-2B. CAS-D, CAS-QL.
Attribute Description: CASD - Natural Science (CASD); CAS - Quantitative Literacy (CASQ); FND - Physical Science (IVB);  PA - Natural Sciences (PA2B); Ohio Transfer 36 Course (TM)

Subject: CHM
Title: Chemistry Mod Soc Laboratory
Description:
111L Chemistry in Modern Society Laboratory (1)

Laboratory course for non-science majors. Students will explore basic chemistry principles that are relevant in everyday life through observation and data analysis. Emphasis will be placed on activities and assessments that develop quantitative reasoning skills and critical thinking with a focus on application to global issues and the development of civic-mindedness. 1 Lab. IVB. PA-2B. CAS-D/LAB, CAS-QL.
Attribute Description: CASD - Natural Science (CASD); CAS - Quantitative Literacy (CASQ); FND - Physical Science (IVB);  PA - Natural Sciences (PA2B); Ohio Transfer 36 Course (TM); Online Asynchronous (ONLA)

Subject: CHM
Title: Chemistry Of Life Processes
Description:
131 Chemistry of Life Processes (4)

Integrated lecture and laboratory course for non-science majors that relate basic inorganic and organic chemical processes to those of biochemistry. Explores the nature of atoms and molecules in terms of simple structures and reactions, and the more complex structures of biochemical molecules and their interactions with living systems. IVB, LAB. PA-2B. CAS-D/LAB. Prerequisite: introductory high school physical science and algebra or CHM 109.
Attribute Description: CASD - Natural Science (CASD); FND - Physical Science (IVB);  PA - Natural Sciences (PA2B)

Title: Chemistry Of Life Processe Lab
Description: None
Attribute Description: FND - Physical Science (IVB);  PA - Natural Sciences (PA2B)

Subject: CHM
Title: College Chemistry
Description:
141 College Chemistry (3)

General chemistry lecture course. Examines the fundamentals of atomic and molecular structure, chemical reactions and stoichiometry, properties of solutions, thermochemistry, gases, and chemical bonding.  Students also develop ideas, experience, methodology, and skills used in the application of scientific methodology. Credit not given for both CHM 141R and 141. IVB. PA-2B. CAS-D. Prerequisite: A math ACT score of 22 (or SAT math sub-score 530) or higher, or completion of MTH 025, or MTH 122 or MTH 125 or MTH 151, or Miami Math Placement Test score of 8 or higher, or permission of instructor; high school chemistry is recommended.
Attribute Description: CASD - Natural Science (CASD); FND - Physical Science (IVB);  PA - Natural Sciences (PA2B); TAG Course (TG); Ohio Transfer 36 Course (TM)

Subject: CHM
Title: College Chemistry
Description:
142 College Chemistry (3)

In this follow-up to CHM 141, students will continue their study of the properties of solutions, thermodynamics, and acids and basis. The course also explores chemical kinetics, chemical equilibrium, coordination chemistry and electrochemistry. Prerequisite: CHM 141. Co-requisite: CHM 145.

Subject: CHM
Title: Col Chemistry For Chem Majors
Description:
142M College Chemistry for Majors (3)

Covers the same content as CHM 142, but assumes interest in chemistry as a major (See CHM 142). Credit not given for both 142M and 142. Prerequisite: CHM 141. Co-requisite: CHM 145M.

Subject: CHM
Title: College Chemistry Laboratory
Description:
144 College Chemistry Laboratory (2)

Presents laboratory exercises to illustrate the fundamental principles of chemistry. An emphasis will be placed on safety, laboratory skills, techniques for simple quantitative measurements and the use of modern instrumentation for data collection and analysis. Students will also gain skills in developing hypotheses, observing chemical phenomena, collecting and sharing data and evaluating results critically. IVB. PA-2B. CAS-D/LAB. Prerequisite or Co-requisite: CHM 141.
Attribute Description: CASD - Natural Science (CASD); FND - Physical Science (IVB);  PA - Natural Sciences (PA2B); Top 25 (T25); TAG Course (TG); Ohio Transfer 36 Course (TM)

Subject: CHM
Title: College Chemistry Laboratory
Description:
145 College Chemistry Laboratory (2)

Presents laboratory exercises to illustrate the fundamental principles of chemistry. In this follow-up to CHM 144, students will continue working on their laboratory skills, using techniques for quantitative measurements and using modern instrumentation for data collection and analysis. Includes chemical kinetics, equilibrium, acids and bases, and electrochemistry. CAS-D/LAB. Prerequisite: CHM 144. Co-requisite: CHM 142 required.

Subject: CHM
Title: Organic Chemistry
Description:
242 Organic Chemistry (3)

Study of stereochemistry and the reaction mechanisms of various types of organic compounds with examples of chemical reactions in biological systems. For premedical and pre-dental students and science majors including majors in chemistry or biochemistry. Prerequisite: CHM 241.

Subject: CHM
Title: Outlines Of Biochemistry
Description:
332 Outlines of Biochemistry (3)

Overview of the major topics of biochemistry. Topics include: biological functions of proteins, carbohydrates, lipids and nucleic acids, central metabolic pathways and controls, and relevant examples in nutrition and health. 3 Lec. CAS-D. Prerequisite: CHM 231, CHM 242, or CHM 252.

Subject: CHM
Title: Outlines Of Biochemistry Lab
Description:
332L Outlines of Biochemistry Lab (1)

Laboratory course designed to enhance the learning experiences in CHM 332.  Emphasis on problem solving and data analysis using basic biochemical experiments. Prerequisite/co-requisite: CHM 332.

Subject: CHM
Title: Analytical Chem. for Majors
Description:
375 Analytical Chemistry for Majors (3)

Instrumental methods of analysis including theory, problem solving, and laboratory experiments applied to real-life samples. Instruction in writing and quantitative literacy. 1 Lec. 2 Lab. ADVW. PA-1C. CAS-Q. CAS-W. Prerequisites: CHM 242 or CHM 252.
Attribute Description: Advanced Writing (ADVW); CAS - Quantitative Literacy (CASQ); CAS - Writing (CASW);  PA - Advanced Writing (PA1C)

Subject: CHM
Title: Polymer Chemistry
Description:
429/529 Polymer Chemistry (2) 

Comprehensive overview and examination of the methods used to synthesize and characterize macromolecules. Both descriptive and mechanistic organic chemistry, as it relates to polymer synthesis, is discussed. The relationship between molecular structure and material properties will be another focus of the course. Prerequisite: CHM 242 or CHM 252 or Graduate Standing.

Subject: CHM
Title: Biophysical Chemistry II
Description:
472/572 Biophysical Chemistry II (3)

Fundamentals of physical biochemistry including thermodynamics, macromolecular structure, kinetics, enzyme kinetics, quantum chemistry, and biophysical spectroscopy. Note: Credit may not be received for both CHM 452 and CHM 472. Prerequisite: MTH 251 or equivalent and CHM 451 or CHM 471.

Subject: CHM
Title: Chemistry In Societal Issues
Description:
491 Chemistry in Societal Issues (3)

Chemistry is involved in many of the societal issues facing this nation. In order to protect the environment, create new energy sources, improve health, and increase consumer product safety, understanding chemistry is critical to the problem-solving process. It is important for students in technical fields to understand the interface between the known chemistry and government regulations, public perception, and legal interpretations. Students critically evaluate and form positions on current issues of national interest. SC. Prerequisite: any 300-level chemistry course.

Subject: CHM
Title: Ind Research Cap In Chemistry
Description:
492 Independent Research Capstone in Chemistry (3)

Students work intensively with instructor to identify a suitable research question; perform research necessary to resolve the question; write a detailed report of the research, results, and the broader scientific and social implications of the overall research project; and communicate the research results to other students and professionals by participation in departmental research presentations and/or participation in a recognized professional meeting. SC. Prerequisite: permission of instructor and department chair, and one semester of CHM 377, 477, 480, or 490 Independent Study.
